content.problemStatement:
Every Christmas, Chef has a peculiar tradition of gifting a permutation PPP of length NNN. For each index iii in the permutation (1≤i≤N)(1 \leq i \leq N)(1≤i≤N), there is an associated value ViV_iVi​.

The value ViV_iVi​ is the number of steps needed to return to index iii when following this path:


Start at index iii.
Let the current index be kkk, then, move to the index PkP_kPk​.
Continue until you return to index iii.

For example, let P=[3,2,5,4,1]P = [3, 2, 5, 4, 1]P=[3,2,5,4,1]. Then, at index 333, the path is 3→5→1→33 \rightarrow 5 \rightarrow 1 \rightarrow 33→5→1→3, then V3=3V_3 = 3V3​=3.
Chef can perform operations to modify the permutation. In one operation, he can:


Swap elements at positions iii and jjj.
The cost of this swap is Vi+VjV_i + V_jVi​+Vj​ (calculated before the swap).
After the swap, values ViV_iVi​ and VjV_jVj​ (and possibly others) will change.
Chef's goal is to transform the permutation to achieve the maximum possible sum of all values (∑i=1NVi)(\sum_{i=1}^N V_i)(∑i=1N​Vi​).

Find the minimum total cost required to achieve the maximum possible sum of values.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the length of the permutation.
The next line contains NNN space separated integers PiP_iPi​, the elements of the permuation.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the minimum total cost required to achieve the maximum possible sum of values.
constraints: 1≤Pi≤N;1 \leq P_i \leq N;1≤Pi​≤N; all values of PiP_iPi​ are distinct.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It can be shown that the permutation already has maximum possible value sum. The value of each element is 333, resulting in a value sum of 999.
Test case 222: For the initial permutation P=[4,1,3,2]P = [4, 1, 3, 2]P=[4,1,3,2], the value array is V=[3,3,1,3]V = [3, 3, 1, 3]V=[3,3,1,3].
Chef can swap indices 222 and 333 for a cost of 3+1=43 + 1 = 43+1=4, to obtain the permutation P=[4,3,1,2]P = [4, 3, 1, 2]P=[4,3,1,2], where value of each element is 444.
